speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Must he needs trouble me in ’t? Hum! ’Bove all others?

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: He might have tried Lord Lucius or Lucullus;

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: And now Ventidius is wealthy too,

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Whom he redeemed from prison. All these

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Owes their estates unto him.

speaker: SERVANT
text: My lord,

speaker: SERVANT
text: They have all been touched and found base metal,

speaker: SERVANT
text: For they have all denied him.

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: How? Have they denied him?

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Has Ventidius and Lucullus denied him,

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: And does he send to me? Three? Humh!

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: It shows but little love or judgment in him.

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Must I be his last refuge? His friends, like physicians,

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Thrive, give him over. Must I take th’ cure upon me?

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Has much disgraced me in ’t. I’m angry at him

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: That might have known my place. I see no sense for ’t

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: But his occasions might have wooed me first;

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: For, in my conscience, I was the first man

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: That e’er received gift from him.

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: And does he think so backwardly of me now

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: That I’ll requite it last? No.

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: So it may prove an argument of laughter

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: To th’ rest, and I ’mongst lords be thought a fool.

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: I’d rather than the worth of thrice the sum

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Had sent to me first, but for my mind’s sake;

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: I’d such a courage to do him good. But now return,

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: And with their faint reply this answer join:

speaker: SEMPRONIUS
text: Who bates mine honor shall not know my coin.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Excellent! Your Lordship’s a goodly villain.

speaker: SERVANT
text: The devil knew not what he did when he made

speaker: SERVANT
text: man politic. He crossed himself by ’t, and I cannot

speaker: SERVANT
text: think but, in the end, the villainies of man will set

speaker: SERVANT
text: him clear. How fairly this lord strives to appear

speaker: SERVANT
text: foul! Takes virtuous copies to be wicked, like those

speaker: SERVANT
text: that under hot ardent zeal would set whole realms

speaker: SERVANT
text: on fire.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Of such a nature is his politic love.

speaker: SERVANT
text: This was my lord’s best hope. Now all are fled,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Save only the gods. Now his friends are dead,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Doors that were ne’er acquainted with their wards

speaker: SERVANT
text: Many a bounteous year must be employed

speaker: SERVANT
text: Now to guard sure their master.

speaker: SERVANT
text: And this is all a liberal course allows:

speaker: SERVANT
text: Who cannot keep his wealth must keep his house.
